claims.claim: Spotify parses episode chapters from the description; requires minimum 3 chapters, first at 00:00, plain-text titles, and supports HH:MM:SS / MM:SS timestamp formats.
Podcasting 2.0 Chapters JSON uses version 1.2.0, startTime in seconds, title field, referenced via the <podcast:chapters type=application/json+chapters> RSS tag.
Apple Podcasts ingests <podcast:transcript> (VTT/SRT) but its auto-generated transcript languages exclude Hebrew; hosted transcripts in any language can still be referenced.
FFmpeg's subtitles filter passes SRTs through libass and supports force_style; libass needs FriBidi + HarfBuzz to render Hebrew RTL correctly.
libass requires FriBidi (bidi resolution) and HarfBuzz (shaping) to render Hebrew/Arabic; without them text renders mirrored.
Heebo is a Hebrew-capable Google Font usable for subtitle rendering.
The macOS Homebrew core formula ffmpeg-full bundles libass and HarfBuzz (FriBidi transitively via libass); the default ffmpeg formula does not, and Homebrew no longer supports --with-* options.

The clip shareability scoring weights are this skill's own heuristic, not external facts: quotable density 40%, emotional peaks 25%, standalone comprehensibility 20%, topic hook 15% (sum 100%). They are tunable in scripts/score_segments.py.
example.com / cdn.example.com are RFC 2606 reserved domains used only as illustrative placeholders in chapter/RSS examples.
